_about: Coverage domains — the fixed set of quality/constraint areas a requirements SET is assessed for. Each domain maps to one specialized coverage judge. Grounded in ISO/IEC 25010:2023, 25012, 25019, ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018, Volere, and domain oracles (OWASP ASVS, WCAG). Archetypes (catalog/project_types/*) provide per-domain *specifics* that layer on top of these generic baselines.

domains.name: Functional suitability & capability completeness
concerns: all specified tasks and user goals covered
success and error/exception behaviour both specified
questions: Is every stated user goal traceable to at least one requirement?
Are required accuracy/precision levels defined where results matter?
Are the unwanted/exception behaviours specified, not just the happy path?

domains.name: Data & information
questions: Are the data entities, schemas and formats specified?
Are retention, archival and deletion rules defined?
Are data-quality thresholds (accuracy/completeness/timeliness) stated?

domains.name: Performance efficiency, scalability & capacity
concerns: resource utilisation (CPU, memory, GPU, network, storage)
scalability / elasticity under load and growth
questions: Are latency/throughput targets quantified for the key operations?
Are resource-utilisation ceilings specified?
Is behaviour under peak load and future growth defined (scaling strategy)?

domains.name: Reliability & resilience
questions: Is an availability target (and its measurement window) stated?
Is behaviour on dependency/hardware failure specified?
Are RTO/RPO and backup/restore requirements defined?

domains.name: Security
questions: Are authN/authZ, session and password/credential policies specified?
Is encryption (at rest and in transit) required and scoped?
Are audit logging and secrets/key management addressed?

domains.name: Safety
questions: Have hazards and their mitigations been identified?
Is a safe state / fail-safe behaviour defined for faults?
Are operator hazard warnings and operational limits specified?

domains.name: Compatibility & interoperability
questions: Are the external interfaces/protocols and their versions specified?
Are data-exchange formats and their compatibility rules defined?
Is coexistence with other systems on the same host/network addressed?

domains.name: Usability & interaction
questions: Are acceptable learning time / workflow-complexity targets stated?
Are safeguards against destructive user actions required?
Are look-and-feel / interaction expectations specified?

domains.name: Accessibility & internationalization
concerns: inclusive design for the target population
questions: Is a WCAG conformance level required?
Are localization/i18n requirements (languages, locales) stated?
Are assistive-technology needs of the user population addressed?

domains.name: Maintainability, testability & observability
questions: Are architectural boundaries defined so parts can change independently?
Are automated-test coverage and diagnostic-logging requirements stated?
Are observability (metrics/tracing) needs specified for operations?

domains.name: Portability & deployment
questions: Are the target platforms/environments and packaging specified?
Are installation/upgrade/rollback requirements defined?
Is the deployment model (e.g. containers, orchestration) stated?

domains.name: Operational & environmental
concerns: the physical/network reality the system must survive (offline, air-gapped, on-prem, intermittent connectivity)
questions: Is the operating environment (connectivity, on-prem/air-gapped, edge) specified?
Are monitoring/alerting/runbook requirements defined?
Is configuration/secret/environment management addressed?

domains.name: Legal, compliance & privacy
concerns: data protection & privacy (GDPR/HIPAA, PII handling, consent)
questions: Are applicable regulations (GDPR/HIPAA/sector) identified with concrete obligations?
Are PII handling, consent, and data-subject rights specified?
Are data-residency/sovereignty constraints stated?

domains.name: Constraints & mandated solutions
questions: Are mandated technologies / prohibited choices stated?
Are licensing constraints on third-party dependencies defined?
Are budget and schedule constraints captured as requirements?

domains.name: Cultural, political & organizational
concerns: human workflows the system must fit
questions: Are organizational policies/governance the system must obey captured?
Are geopolitical constraints (e.g. data routing, sanctions) addressed?
Does the system fit the existing human workflows and roles?

domains.name: Quality-in-use & stakeholder outcomes
questions: Are the intended user outcomes/success metrics defined?
Is acceptable effort/time-to-outcome specified?
Are risks to users (economic/health/environmental) considered?
